response:
In C# it is possible to estimate the time it takes for a UDP packet to reach a server  
using the System.Net.Sockets namespace  
you can send a UDP packet and then wait for a response  
however UDP is connectionless and does not guarantee delivery  
so you cannot always expect a response  
you can use the SendTo method to send the packet  
then use the Stopwatch class to measure the time  
you can start the stopwatch before sending the packet  
and stop it when you receive a response  
this will give you the round trip time  
but you are only measuring one direction  
you can send a packet and then wait for a reply  
if no reply is received you cannot determine the time  
you can also use the Ping class to check the round trip time  
but it uses ICMP not UDP  
so it is not exactly the same  
you can use the Stopwatch class to measure the time  
between sending and receiving a response  
this will give you the time taken for the packet to reach the server  
and return  
you can use the Socket class to send and receive UDP packets  
you can also use the NetworkInformation namespace  
to get information about network latency  
but it may not be accurate  
you can use the System.Diagnostics namespace  
to measure the time taken for the packet to be sent  
and received  
you can use the SendTo method to send the packet  
and the ReceiveFrom method to receive the response  
you can use the Stopwatch class to measure the time  
between sending and receiving the response  
this will give you the time taken for the packet to reach the server  
and return  
you can use the Stopwatch class to measure the time  
taken for the packet to be sent  
and received  
you can use the SendTo method to send the packet  
and the ReceiveFrom method to receive the response  
you can use the Stopwatch class to measure the time  
between sending and receiving the response  
this will give you the time taken for the packet to reach the server  
and return
